How can I get a Library Card? Anyone can get a Peabody Institute Library card at any of our 3 locations! Just show library staff a photo ID and proof of residence, and fill out a short application. Children 12 and under must have a parent or guardian permission and signature.
You can also sign up for an e-card by clicking here!
Can I get a Library Card if I don't live in Peabody? Yes! We just need to see proof of your address. If you live in another NOBLE library community, you can use your card at the Peabody Institute Library, but you can only have one card in the NOBLE network. Please be aware that some online resources are only available for Peabody residents.
Can I check out or pick up items if I forgot my Library Card? Yes! You can use another form of ID to confirm your identity and account.
Can I pick up holds for someone else? If you would like to pick up items for another person, that person must come into the library in person, show proof of identity, and state that you are permitted to pick up their items for them.
Can you tell me what items are checked out on my child's card? No, but we will be happy to call, email, or mail a list of those items to the card holder.
Can you tell me what items I have checked out or placed on hold in the past? Library staff does not have access to this information. However, you may enable “Checkout History” or “Holds History” by logging into your account at http://peabody.noblenet.org/, clicking on “Account Preferences”, and “Search and History Preferences”. By default, these History options are NOT enabled. Once enabled, they will only keep track of what you check or place on hold from that day forwar
Where can I view what items I currently have checked out and/or any fines on my account? Ask a librarian for an online account PIN! Logging in at http://peabody.noblenet.org/ using your library card number and PIN, you can:
Place and manage item holds.
View and renew items borrowed on your card.
Pay fines using a credit card.
Update or change your log-in information, email address, and phone number.
Manage your account preferences for library notifications, check out history, and more!
Sign Up for our Weekly Newsletter
card_page.eligibility_source_phrase: How can I get a Library Card? Anyone can get a Peabody Institute Library card at any of our 3 locations! Just show library staff a photo

pass_page.policy_text:
To limit museum types being displayed, check the corresponding categories:
To select a museum, click “Details/Reserve”, then select which Pass to reserve.
{"@context": "http://schema.org","@type": "CivicStructure","address": "308 Congress Street, Boston, MA 02210","logo": "https://s3.amazonaws.com/ai-assets/museum-logos/ma-boston-childrens-museum_3_md.jpg","telephone": "617-426-6500","name": "Boston Children&apos;s Museum","url": "","description": "Founded in 1913 by the Science Teachers&apos; Bureau, the Boston Children&apos;s Museum is the second oldest children&apos;s museum in the world and one of the few that maintain a collection. Designed for both children and families, the Museum exhibits focus on science, culture, environmental awareness, health & fitness, and the arts, emphasizing hands-on engagement and learning through experience, and employing play as a tool to spark the inherent creativity, curiosity, and imagination of children."} Boston Children's Museum 308 Congress Street, Boston, MA 02210617-426-6500http://www.bostonchildrensmuseum.org/[Details/Reserve]
Boston Children's Museum Founded in 1913 by the Science Teachers' Bureau, the Boston Children's Museum is the second oldest children's museum in the world and one of the few that maintain a collection. Designed for both children and families, the Museum exhibits focus on science, culture, environmental awareness, health & fitness, and the arts, emphasizing hands-on engagement and learning through experience, and employing play as a tool to spark the inherent creativity, curiosity, and imagination of children.
Categories: Children, Crafts, Family, Technology
Pass Type: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
Pass Benefits Please check Boston Children's Museum website for updated information before visiting.  .
Pass admits up to 4 people at half price.
Children under  12 months  free
This is an electronic pass that will be sent to your email.
Advance tickets/reservations are highly recommended, especially on weekends, holidays, school vacation weeks.
Reserve a Pass Reserve a Pass
{"@context": "http://schema.org","@type": "CivicStructure","address": "83 Pine Street, Unit F, Peabody, MA 01960","logo": "https://s3.amazonaws.com/ai-assets/museum-logos/ma-the-childrens-piazza_220_md.jpg","telephone": "978-817-2809","name": "The Children&apos;s Piazza","url": "","description": "Located in Peabody, MA, The Children&apos;s Piazza is a space is designed for young children&mdash;aged 0 to 6&mdash;to learn through unstructured play. Organized to optimize social, cognitive and motor development, areas include a large room with a climbing structure, slides, and swings; an arts and crafts room; a room with a train table; a lunch area with appropriately sized furniture; a glow-in-the-dark planet room; among others. There is a coffee shop and free Wi-Fi on the premises. Please check the website for the requirements of entry."} The Children's Piazza 83 Pine Street, Unit F, Peabody, MA 01960978-817-2809https://www.thechildrenspiazza.com/[Details/Reserve]
The Children's Piazza Located in Peabody, MA, The Children's Piazza is a space is designed for young children—aged 0 to 6—to learn through unstructured play. Organized to optimize social, cognitive and motor development, areas include a large room with a climbing structure, slides, and swings; an arts and crafts room; a room with a train table; a lunch area with appropriately sized furniture; a glow-in-the-dark planet room; among others. There is a coffee shop and free Wi-Fi on the premises. Please check the website for the requirements of entry.
Categories: Children, Family
Pass Benefits This pass provides 50% off regular admission for one family (immediate family/household only).
Weekends  and the following holidays are excluded: Thanksgiving, Christmas, New Year's Eve, New Year's Day, and the 4th of July.
Please visit The Children's Piazza website (thechildrenspiazza.com) to make a reservation prior to your visit. Reservations required for entry. Pass only valid on date reserved.
The email confirmation sent to you serves as your discount pass. Please present this pass (either printed or on your mobile device) upon entry to the Children's Piazza. Library staff may print this pass for you upon request.
{"@context": "http://schema.org","@type": "CivicStructure","address": "145 Redstone Hill, Sterling, MA 01564","logo": "https://s3.amazonaws.com/ai-assets/museum-logos/ma-davis-farmland_47_md.jpg","telephone": "978-422-6666","name": "Davis Farmland","url": "","description": "Davis Farmland is a seven-generation, working family farm, continuously in operation since 1846 in Sterling, MA. Starting in 1996, Davis Farmland has provided opportunities for discovery and play to families with children 12 years old and younger. It has a Waterspray Park; Imagine-Acres; Adventureland; interactions with farm animals; and a &ldquo;Mega Maze&rdquo; in the fall. It is also home to the largest sanctuary of endangered farm animals in North America. Special events include hands-on reptile shows. Hours change with the season. Shop with food and souvenirs on the premises."} Davis Farmland 145 Redstone Hill, Sterling, MA 01564978-422-6666https://www.davisfarmland.com/[Details/Reserve]
Davis Farmland Davis Farmland is a seven-generation, working family farm, continuously in operation since 1846 in Sterling, MA. Starting in 1996, Davis Farmland has provided opportunities for discovery and play to families with children 12 years old and younger. It has a Waterspray Park; Imagine-Acres; Adventureland; interactions with farm animals; and a “Mega Maze” in the fall. It is also home to the largest sanctuary of endangered farm animals in North America. Special events include hands-on reptile shows. Hours change with the season. Shop with food and souvenirs on the premises.
Categories: Children, Family, Nature, Zoo
Pass Type: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
Pass Benefits Admission tickets are purchased at the gate with library pass. This pass admits up to 6 guests to Davis Farmland or Davis Mega Maze with a savings of $10 OFF the admission rate per person. 
Davis Farmland Discovery Farm is open April 16th-November 1st - dates and rates change with the season. Davis Farmland and Davis Mega Maze are only open WEATHER PERMITTING. ALWAYS call or check the web before heading out. Visit DavisFarmland.com for the most up-to-date operating schedule. 
NOTICE: Adults MUST be accompanied by at least 1 child 12 years or younger when visiting Davis Farmland. 
This is a physical pass that must be picked up at the Main Library.
{"@context": "http://schema.org","@type": "CivicStructure","address": "115 Derby Street, Salem, MA 01970","logo": "https://s3.amazonaws.com/ai-assets/museum-logos/ma-the-house-of-seven-gables_84_md.jpg","telephone": "978-744-0991","name": "The House of Seven Gables","url": "","description": "The House of the Seven Gables, one of the oldest surviving timber-framed mansion houses in continental North America, was the setting of Nathaniel Hawthorne&apos;s famous 1851 novel of the same name. The historic site which includes the house (a National Historic Landmark District) includes a guided tour of the Turner-Ingersoll Mansion (the House of the Seven Gables) and visits to the Nathaniel Hawthorne Birthplace, the Colonial Revival seaside gardens, and the Counting House, with a maritime discovery zone called &ldquo;Kids&apos; Cove at The Gables.&rdquo; (An audio tour of the gardens, grounds, and waterfront is available through the UniGuide App.) Lectures and seasonal living history labs are scheduled throughout the year, and a changing annual exhibition examines various aspects of the institution&apos;s 350 years of history. There is a gift shop on the premises. The website has virtual field trips and interactive exhibitions that can provide helpful background information before a visit."} The House of Seven Gables 115 Derby Street, Salem, MA 01970978-744-0991https://www.7gables.org/[Details/Reserve]
The House of Seven Gables The House of the Seven Gables, one of the oldest surviving timber-framed mansion houses in continental North America, was the setting of Nathaniel Hawthorne's famous 1851 novel of the same name. The historic site which includes the house (a National Historic Landmark District) includes a guided tour of the Turner-Ingersoll Mansion (the House of the Seven Gables) and visits to the Nathaniel Hawthorne Birthplace, the Colonial Revival seaside gardens, and the Counting House, with a maritime discovery zone called “Kids' Cove at The Gables.” (An audio tour of the gardens, grounds, and waterfront is available through the UniGuide App.) Lectures and seasonal living history labs are scheduled throughout the year, and a changing annual exhibition examines various aspects of the institution's 350 years of history. There is a gift shop on the premises. The website has virtual field trips and interactive exhibitions that can provide helpful background information before a visit.
Categories: Children, Family, Science
